evidence: Each frame centers on the single amber probe arrow and the lilac score bars responding to it, with the periwinkle candidates as supporting context.
The elements form one coherent system: one probe, four candidate arrows, and four score bars, well under the flood threshold.
Labels A/B/C/D sit on their arrows, 'probe' rides the amber arrow, and numeric readouts sit on their bars with no legend box.
On-screen text is limited to the 'Predict' prompt card and short object labels/readouts, each tied to a visible referent.
All labels, numeric readouts, and the prediction card text are rendered at readable size against the dark sky.
All arrows, bars, and the prediction card sit fully inside the frame with adequate margins.
When the probe aligns near candidate D the amber arrow slightly overlaps the 'D' label, but no unrelated content is rendered illegible.
Probe is amber (attention), candidate direction arrows are periwinkle, and score bars are lilac (parameter/score), consistent across frames.
Every element—probe, candidates, bars, readouts, prediction card—carries propositional content with no decoration.
The prediction card precedes the sweep, the amber probe rotates through directions, and lilac bars climb with integer readouts (3/5/4/0, 4/1/-2/-5) as promised.
Score bars sit directly beneath their candidate arrows and readouts sit on the bars, keeping related items together.
Across frames the probe angle and bar heights change continuously, reading as a sweep rather than cuts.
The composition keeps generous margins with the arrow cluster above and the bar chart below in a clear reading order.
